# Law

- [HB 110 (2021) - withholding relief for employers, refunds for employees](hb-110-2021.md) - The 2021 budget bill split the pandemic rule - employers could keep withholding to the office city through 2021, but employees could claim refunds based on where they actually worked.
- [HB 197 (2020), Section 29 - the pandemic withholding rule](hb-197-2020.md) - The emergency law that deemed every WFH day a day worked at the principal place of work, in force 2020-03-09 through 2021-07-18, upheld in Schaad v. Alder.
- [ORC Chapter 718 - Municipal Income Tax](orc-718.md) - The state framework - key sections for refunds (718.19), limitations (718.12), withholding (718.03), and the 20-day occasional entrant rule (718.011).
- [Schaad v. Alder (2024) - the 2020 refund question, answered](schaad-v-alder.md) - Ohio Supreme Court, 2024-Ohio-525, decided 2024-02-14 (5-2) - HB 197 §29 was constitutional, so Ohio residents get no 2020 WFH refunds; out-of-state workers (Morsy) were different.
